source: user request 2026-05-08 — 'i want to have the obsidain graph view in vs code'
title: VS Code Foam extension config for vault graph view
what_failed: Vault knowledge graph (PI.md, vault/notes/, CHECKPOINTS, tickets) was visible only in standalone Obsidian — separate window from VS Code. Ash wanted it inside the IDE he codes in.
suggested_fix: Add .vscode/extensions.json recommending foam.foam-vscode (Roam-style backlinks + graph view + daily notes). Add .vscode/settings.json with foam.workspace.includeGlobs covering PI.md, vault/, CHECKPOINTS/, docs/, and ignoring docs/_archive/, pi_env/, data/, logs/. Document the setup in docs/vscode-setup.md so a fresh checkout (where .vscode/ is gitignored) knows what to do.
fix_applied: Created .vscode/extensions.json (foam, python, pylance, markdown-all-in-one, yaml). Created .vscode/settings.json with python interpreter, pytest config, file/search/watcher excludes, and full Foam configuration. Wrote docs/vscode-setup.md with install steps, what-Foam-gives-you, and template configs for fresh checkouts.
tests: Manual — Foam extension installation is interactive. Verification steps documented in docs/vscode-setup.md.
